{"step_by_step_thinking": "Zinc eugenol cement contains eugenol, which is substituted by EBA (ethylene bisaminomethyl ether) to increase the strength of the cement. Polycarboxylate cement does not involve the substitution of eugenol by EBA. Both zinc eugenol cement and polycarboxylate cement can form chemical bonds to the tooth structure. Chelation is a characteristic of glass ionomer cements, not polycarboxylate cements. Polyacrylic acid is a component of polycarboxylate cement, but it is not explicitly mentioned as a liquid in the provided information.", "answer_choice": "B"}
